creator: numisemis@yahoo.com
text:
--- Volker <volker@vwsoft.com> wrote:
> Simun: Did you try your patch and did you get if_aue to work with
> that NIC?
Yes I did.
I'm using it instead of the built-in PCI NIC right now, works great.
This is what dmesg says:

aue0: ADMtek USB To LAN Converter, rev 2.00/1.01, addr 6
miibus0: <MII bus> on aue0
ukphy0: <Generic IEEE 802.3u media interface> on miibus0
ukphy0:  10baseT, 10baseT-FDX, 100baseTX, 100baseTX-FDX, auto
aue0: Ethernet address: 00:05:1b:9f:6c:03
aue0: if_start running deferred for Giant
aue0: link state changed to DOWN
aue0: link state changed to UP

> I've seen a similar patch for NetBSD, but yesterday I tried a
> similar NIC (vid 0x0a46, pid 0x8515) and after patching if_aue for
> this vid/pid, the driver fails to initialize with MII read timeout
> problems.
Does your kernel have "device miibus" compiled in?
